title: actaea elata
intro: Actaea elata, also known as tall bugbane, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. The plant contains sesquiterpenes, particularly actein and cimicifugine, which exhibit medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Actaea elata has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders, such as hot flashes and night sweats, due to its potential to balance hormonal fluctuations and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Actaea elata, often in the form of dried roots or tinctures, are commonly used in folk medicine to support female reproductive health and alleviate various other health issues.
